forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KY0HNVTKN7ZJ85H8ZJN2WJD5
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a880
+title: Stream actual review findings + verdicts over MCP notifications/message as they resolve
+---
+## What
+
+Stream the review engine's actual results over MCP as they are produced — not just progress ticks — so an MCP client (e.g. the main agent driving a review) can start resolving findings in parallel while the run is still going. Builds on the ^jn2wjd5 event channel/bridge (depends on it).
+
+Ground truth about finality (verified in code): fan-out validator tasks run independently (`collect_fan_out`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`), and verification is per-finding and independent — `verify_findings` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs`) runs each candidate through the deterministic `run_guard` and then its own adversarial verify task, pipelined on the same pool; `synthesize` only exact-dedups, it never revises verdicts. So each finding's data is final at two well-defined moments: its raw content when its validator task completes, and its confirmed/refuted verdict when its verify task resolves. Stream both.
+
+Transport: `notifications/progress` carries only progress/total/message — wrong channel for content. Use MCP logging notifications (`notifications/message`), which carry arbitrary structured `data`; the peer path already exists (`send_mcp_log` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_registry.rs` / `peer.notify_logging_message`). Full payloads always — never truncate a finding.
+
+Implementation shape:
+
+1. **Engine events** — extend `ReviewProgressEvent` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`, added by ^jn2wjd5) with two content-carrying variants:
+   - `Findings { validator: String, findings: Vec<Finding> }` — emitted in `collect_fan_out` at each validator task's resolution with the task's parsed, validator-tagged findings (empty vec = that validator came back clean; failed task = no Findings event, the existing PairDone/failure accounting covers it).
+   - `Verdict { finding: Finding, confirmed: bool, reason: String }` — emitted as each candidate's verdict resolves: guard refutations in/after `run_guard`, agent verdicts in `collect_verify`/`verify_findings` (`verify.rs`). Requires threading the existing optional progress sender from `run_review_over_agent` (`drive.rs`) into the verify stage (it already reaches the fleet stage from ^jn2wjd5).
+2. **MCP bridge** — in `spawn_review_progress_bridge` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs`, from ^jn2wjd5), map the two new variants to `LoggingMessageNotificationParam` with structured `data` (e.g. `{"kind": "review.findings", "validator": …, "findings": [full Finding JSON…]}` / `{"kind": "review.verdict", "finding": …, "confirmed": …, "reason": …}`, logger `"review"`, level Info) sent via the peer; keep progress-tick variants flowing to `notifications/progress` as today. When only an in-process `progress_sink` exists (no peer), skip logging notifications — the sink contract is progress params.
+3. Document the streamed message shapes in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/description.md` — note events are per-validator granular while the final report exact-dedups across validators (dedup, never retraction).
+
+Subtasks:
+- [ ] `Findings` + `Verdict` variants on `ReviewProgressEvent`; emit from `collect_fan_out`, guard refutations, and `collect_verify`
+- [ ] Thread the progress sender into `verify_findings` (drive.rs → verify.rs)
+- [ ] Bridge maps content variants → `notifications/message` with full structured payloads (progress ticks unchanged)
+- [ ] Document message shapes in description.md
+- [ ] Tests (below)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] During a review with a live peer, each validator task's completion produces one `notifications/message` carrying every parsed finding for that validator, before the final tool result arrives
+- [ ] Each finding's verdict (confirmed or refuted, with reason — both guard- and agent-decided) produces its own `notifications/message` as it resolves
+- [ ] Streamed finding payloads are complete Finding JSON (file, rule, validator, claim/description fields) — no truncation, no summaries
+- [ ] The final `ReviewReport` is unchanged, and the union of streamed confirmed verdicts matches the report's findings modulo synthesize's exact dedup
+- [ ] With no peer (in-process sink only, or neither), zero logging notifications are emitted and existing behavior is unchanged
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Fleet/verify unit tests (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet/tests.rs`, `verify.rs` test mod, scripted agents): assert `Findings` events per completed validator task (including a clean/empty one) and `Verdict` events for a guard-refuted, an agent-confirmed, and an agent-refuted candidate
+- [ ] Extend `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/tests/review_progress_notifications_test.rs` (or a sibling e2e): client also captures `notifications/message`; assert finding + verdict messages arrive with full structured data during the run and that confirmed streamed findings match the final report
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators review` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(review)'` — green, scripted agents only
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

   text: 'Picked up. Research done: verified collect_fan_out (fleet.rs) already carries progress + tags findings via parse_task_response before extend, so Findings emits there on Ok(parsed) (empty=clean, Err=no event). verify_findings (verify.rs) needs a new progress param threaded from run_review (synthesize.rs, which already has progress). Verdict emits: guard-refuted via loop over run_guard''s `refuted`, agent verdicts in the collect loop. Bridge (review_op.rs run_review_progress_mapping) will route content variants (Findings/Verdict) to notifications/message via peer (LoggingMessageNotificationParam, logger "review", Info); progress-tick variants unchanged. Plan: review_progress_param -> Option (None for content), review_content_log_param -> Option<LoggingMessageNotificationParam>; thread Option<Arc<Peer>> into mapping (peer=None => skip content logging, sink-only contract). Client-side receipt proven by extending review_progress_stdio_test.rs (on_logging_message capture). TDD next.'
   timestamp: 2026-07-21T06:52:28.293560+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01ky1rq2gff5h4rbfr8nch2p03
+  text: |-
+    Implemented + all gates green. Changes: (1) ReviewProgressEvent gained Findings{validator,findings} and Verdict{finding,confirmed,reason} content variants (fleet.rs); Findings emitted in collect_fan_out on Ok(parsed) (empty=clean, failed task=no event); Verdict emitted in verify_findings for guard refutations (loop over run_guard's refuted) and agent verdicts (collect loop). (2) Threaded progress sender into verify_findings (drive->synthesize->verify). (3) Bridge (review_op.rs): review_progress_param now returns Option (None for content, never moves wire counter); new review_content_log_param maps content variants to LoggingMessageNotificationParam {kind:review.findings|review.verdict, logger "review", Info, full untruncated Finding JSON}; run_review_progress_mapping takes Option<Arc<Peer>>, routes content to notifications/message via peer only (no peer=in-process sink => skipped). (4) description.md documents both message shapes + per-validator-granular/dedup-not-retraction note.
+
+    E2E: extended the STDIO e2e review_progress_stdio_test.rs (not http) — CapturingClient now overrides on_logging_message; asserts client-side receipt of full findings JSON + confirmed/refuted verdicts, and report ⊆ streamed-confirmed (modulo synthesize file:line dedup). Proves the new channel over a real byte-stream transport via the client's message handler. Note: in the hermetic run (SAH_DISABLE_EMBEDDING) the duplicates/callers probes don't hit, so CLAIM_DUP is guard-refuted and CLAIM_GUARD_HERRING default-refuted here; the e2e asserts probe-independent outcomes (secret confirmed, red-herring refuted) while the guard-vs-agent split is pinned by the verify.rs unit test.
+
+    Gates: validators review 161 passed; tools -E test(review) 50 passed (both progress e2es); fmt; clippy -D warnings clean; cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean. Left in doing.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-21T07:19:47.983545+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KY0HNVTKN7ZJ85H8ZJN2WJD5
 position_column: doing

   timestamp: 2026-07-21T07:19:47.983545+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01ky1s42t53v2tyc48w3sjdxnx
+  text: 'Adversarial double-check: PASS on the two-channel split, no-truncation, send-before-result ordering, emission sites, and style — returned REVISE for one low-severity gap: the content channel passed context.peer unconditionally, so in the contract-permitted both-sink-and-peer config content would go to the peer, ignoring the documented sink-takes-priority rule. Fixed: spawn_review_progress_bridge now gates the content peer on sink absence (content_peer = sink.is_some() ? None : peer), so a sink transport is progress-params-only (no content on sink, none leaked to peer). Strengthened a_token_with_a_sink test to send a content event and assert it never reaches the progress sink; refined description.md to state content rides the peer transport only. Re-ran: tools review_op + a_token_with_a_sink + stdio e2e all green (23 passed); clippy -D warnings clean. Task remains in doing, ready for /review.'
+  timestamp: 2026-07-21T07:26:54.277060+00:00

-  text: 'Adversarial double-check: PASS on the two-channel split, no-truncation, send-before-result ordering, emission sites, and style — returned REVISE for one low-severity gap: the content channel passed context.peer unconditionally, so in the contract-permitted both-sink-and-peer config content would go to the peer, ignoring the documented sink-takes-priority rule. Fixed: spawn_review_progress_bridge now gates the content peer on sink absence (content_peer = sink.is_some() ? None : peer), so a sink transport is progress-params-only (no content on sink, none leaked to peer). Strengthened a_token_with_a_sink test to send a content event and assert it never reaches the progress sink; refined description.md to state content rides the peer transport only. Re-ran: tools review_op + a_token_with_a_sink + stdio e2e all green (23 passed); clippy -D warnings clean. Task remains in doing, ready for /review.'

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] During a review with a live peer, each validator task's completion produces one `notifications/message` carrying every parsed finding for that validator, before the final tool result arrives
-- [ ] Each finding's verdict (confirmed or refuted, with reason — both guard- and agent-decided) produces its own `notifications/message` as it resolves
-- [ ] Streamed finding payloads are complete Finding JSON (file, rule, validator, claim/description fields) — no truncation, no summaries
-- [ ] The final `ReviewReport` is unchanged, and the union of streamed confirmed verdicts matches the report's findings modulo synthesize's exact dedup
-- [ ] With no peer (in-process sink only, or neither), zero logging notifications are emitted and existing behavior is unchanged
